summary: High‑quality soundboard capture of a solid 1970 show with a rare acoustic set, standout Black Peter and The Other One, marred only by slight pitch drift and occasional pops.
review: The performance is praised for its intimate acoustic set, powerful Pigpen moments, and the intensity of The Other One into Lovelight, with Black Peter highlighted as a highlight. The soundboard source is generally good, though one reviewer notes a half‑step low tempo and another mentions some pops and static; track continuity has been improved in this release.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show 4‑5 stars, highlighting strong musical moments, while recording issues are minor (pitch drift, occasional pops) resulting in a solid overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Slight half‑step low/slow pitch on this version, some pops and stray static, but track splits are clean (China Cat/Sugar Mason, Good Lovin' drums) and The Other One presented as a single track.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Shows a balanced flow with an early acoustic set that adds intimacy, followed by classic electric numbers; the second half builds intensity culminating in The Other One into Lovelight.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for lyric improvisation on Friend of the Devil and shining in the acoustic setting.
band_member_comments.Drums: Noted that drums in Good Lovin' are presented without interruption, improving the listening experience.
